title: Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii
intro: Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii, also known as the Indian polypody fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the Indian polypody fern is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii is classified under the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Selliguea and the species Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii. This species is a type of fern that is native to the tropical regions of Asia. Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii is also known by its synonyms, including Polypodium fraser-jenkinsii. The classification of Selliguea fraser-jenkinsii is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.
